notes: Assertion texts are the canonical source of truth; graders in harness/grade.py key off them. Update in tandem.

evals.name: returning-user-vague
evals.prompt: Hey, I'm coming back to this project after a two-week break. I honestly can't remember where we left off - what should I work on next?
evals.expected_output: Skill walks the decision tree. If docs/what-next.md exists and fingerprints match, uses the cache. Otherwise analyses the repo, detects PM framework, reads the task store, prioritises with weighted heuristic, presents top 3 via AskUserQuestion. No prose prompts, no silent rewrites.

assertions.text: Skill reads backlog.md rather than re-analysing the repo from scratch
Top three tasks are surfaced and ranked by the weighted heuristic
AUTH-001 is ranked #1 (it has p1 + blocks others)
SEC-001 is in the top three (security +35 weight)
The skill records or references AskUserQuestion as the prompting mechanism (not prose)
No agent is spawned before the user picks from the top three
AUTH-002 is NOT in the top-three (implicitly blocked by AUTH-001 per iteration-2 -15 penalty)

evals.name: project-manager-present
evals.expected_output: Delegation rule fires: project-manager detected, no backlog.md created, docs/plans/*.md parsed, top 3 specific plan todos surfaced (IDs like `auth P1-T3`) alongside /review-tasks and /continue-tasks escape-hatch options.

assertions.text: Skill detects pm_framework: project-manager (docs/features/ + docs/plans/ both present)
Skill does NOT create a backlog.md file
Skill recommends delegating to /continue-tasks or /review-tasks
docs/what-next.md is written with pm_framework: project-manager
Top tasks surfaced are specific plan task IDs (not placeholders) — at least one P1-T or P2-T ID appears

evals.name: bare-repo-bootstrap
evals.prompt: /what-next please help me figure out what to do in this repo
evals.expected_output: In a bare repo, skill detects no framework, offers four bootstrap options via AskUserQuestion, infers area prefixes from top-level folders (AUTH/UI/API), confirms the area map before creating backlog.md, captures infra-gap observations (no test runner / no CI / no lint).

assertions.text: Skill detects pm_framework: none (no task store, no project-manager convention)
User is offered four bootstrap options: /project-manager, create backlog.md, code analysis, manual input
Inferred area map includes AUTH, UI, API prefixes
Area map is presented to the user for confirmation before first backlog item
No backlog.md is written until the user's choice has been recorded
Observations list captures at least one infrastructure gap (no test runner / no CI / no linter)

evals.name: update-flow
evals.expected_output: Update flow refreshes fingerprints (regardless of cached values), reconciles backlog against current files, marks tasks referencing deleted files as stale (never auto-closes them), prints a one-line diff summary, excludes stale tasks from the top-three via the iteration-2 -25 penalty.

assertions.text: Skill recomputes fingerprints for all tracked files regardless of cached values
Stale task LEGACY-001 (references deleted file) is marked stale, NOT auto-closed
A short diff summary is printed (areas/tasks added or archived)
docs/what-next.md is rewritten with fresh fingerprints and updated date
After refresh, the skill proceeds to present top-three via AskUserQuestion
LEGACY-001 is NOT in the top-three (stale -25 penalty applied in iteration-2)
